// C10: Models/Data
instance StaticBuildingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predefined geometric and structural model representing the physical building asset (e.g., BIM model)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEnabler, DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance DynamicBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model enriched with device-specific configurations (sensors, actuators, controllers)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEnabler, DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model representing building control systems (HVAC, lighting, security) with operational logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEnabler, ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data including sensor inputs, environmental conditions, and system performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted, BuildingHumidityDataTransmitted,
                  EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted,
                  OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted,
                  HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted, ElectricalSystemDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical operational data for trend analysis and predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance DeviceConfigurationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Device-specific configurations including sensor/actuator settings and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler, ModelTransformationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated model transformations to extract structured representations from BIM and generate deployable DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionService, VisualizationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Mechanisms to combine static models with runtime data for dynamic virtual representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical data collection, storage, and processing for large infrastructure datasets"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Continuous synchronization mechanisms ensuring data consistency between physical and digital systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runtime configuration management enabling dynamic adjustments to device configurations and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService, OptimizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Advanced data analysis for forecasting system behavior, failures, and optimization opportunities"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D representations of the physical asset and its data for immersive monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance CollaborationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multi-stakeholder collaboration tools supporting shared access to DT models and data across AECO domains"
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of physical asset performance (e.g., energy consumption, structural integrity, environmental conditions)"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimePerformanceInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight, SystemHealthBenchmarkInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for system behavior, failures, or optimization opportunities"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, EnergyDemandForecastInsight, LifecycleCostEstimationInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for improving efficiency, reducing costs, or enhancing performance"
    DTDFVocab:provides CostReductionStrategyInsight, ResourceAllocationOptimizationInsight, DesignRefinementSuggestionInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated or semi-automated interventions based on DT insights (e.g., adjusting HVAC systems)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ClosedLoopAdjustmentAction, AutomatedMaintenanceTriggerAction, DynamicReconfigurationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D representations of the physical asset and its data"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveBuildingWalkthroughInsight, DataOverlayVisualizationInsight, ScenarioSimulationInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enhanced decision-making through DT-generated insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides RiskAssessmentToolInsight, ComplianceMonitoringInsight, InvestmentPrioritizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

]

]

]

]

]

    base:desc "'What-if' analysis capabilities for evaluating potential system changes"
]

]

]

// C1: Architecture Components
instance DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized architecture acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital systems"
]

instance BuildingControlEngineerRole : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role responsible for deploying device models and defining control logic within BIM"
]

instance FacilityManagerRole : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role interacting with deployed DTs for real-time monitoring and operational management"
]

    base:desc "Role extending DT capabilities via service integration and modular component development"
]

instance ModularServiceExtensionPoint : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Standardized extension points enabling plug-and-play integration of custom services and analytics"
]

instance HierarchicalRoleInteraction : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role-based hierarchical structure facilitating coordination between engineers, managers, and framework"
]

instance CentralizedDataHub : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Central repository for synchronized data exchange between physical and digital twin instances"
]

instance Environment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin framework environment for building infrastructure management"
    base:contains DigitalTwinFramework, BuildingControlEngineerRole,
               FacilityManagerRole, DigitalTwinEngineerRole,
               ModularServiceExtensionPoint, HierarchicalRoleInteraction,
               CentralizedDataHub
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system enables **real-time control commands** from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed by **Facility Managers** via the deployed DT interface. **Command types** include **adjustments to HVAC setpoints, lighting schedules, security protocols, and energy optimization directives**, derived from analytics or manual overrides. **Control message formats** are standardized to ensure compatibility with building automation systems (e.g., BACnet, LonWorks, or proprietary protocols). **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied to be part of the **runtime configuration management**, ensuring only authorized commands (e.g., from authenticated Facility Managers or DT Engineers) are executed. **Feedback systems** provide **closed-loop confirmation** (e.g., acknowledgment signals from physical devices) to validate command execution, while **conflict resolution** is handled via the **centralized DT Framework**, which arbitrates competing commands (e.g., prioritizing safety overrides over energy-saving directives). **Command execution confirmation** is achieved through **bidirectional data exchange**, where the physical system’s response (e.g., sensor feedback post-adjustment) is looped back to the DT for validation. The system’s **modular architecture** allows for **custom service integration** to extend control logic (e.g., AI-driven predictive maintenance triggers)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency for critical operational monitoring and control. The framework supports dynamic operational needs, suggesting adaptive time scales for different services (e.g., high-frequency sensor data for building control vs. lower-frequency analytics for facility management). Temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing adjustments to synchronization frequencies without full redeployment, though exact latency thresholds or granularity specifications are not explicitly quantified in the provided documents. The system bridges static BIM representations with dynamic operational requirements, implying a hybrid time-scale model where historical (BIM) and real-time (DT) data coexist for life-cycle management."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture pattern** where system components are organized into a **life-cycle pipeline** (enriched BIM models → operational DTs). Orchestration is achieved through **standardized extension points** in the modular framework, enabling dynamic integration of custom services and analytics. Resource management is implicit via **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without redeployment, while system-wide coordination is centralized through the **DT Framework hub**, which facilitates bidirectional data exchange. The constellation leverages **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring/control and **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** to ensure scalability, interoperability, and maintainability across heterogeneous components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling & Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment (e.g., via **F1** in Table III), creating a unified semantic foundation for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation & Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **Deployment & Synchronization**: Real-time data exchange ensures continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling dynamic updates.
  4. **Operation & Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT for **monitoring system states** and **operating physical infrastructure**, while DT Engineers extend functionality via custom services.
  5. **Evolution & Extension**: The modular architecture supports **runtime integration of custom services and analytics**, allowing the DT to adapt to changing operational requirements (e.g., via standardized extension points). Governance is implicit through **stakeholder-specific roles** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and **use-case-driven requirements** (Table II)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity requirements** by enabling **Building Control Engineers** to define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, ensuring semantic alignment between physical and digital representations. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** from enriched BIM models, minimizing manual intervention and reducing human-induced errors. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via **real-time data exchange** mechanisms that synchronize physical and digital states, though explicit quantification methods (e.g., probabilistic models or error bounds) are not detailed. **Error detection and correction** are facilitated by the framework’s **modular architecture**, allowing custom services to integrate **diagnostic analytics** or **anomaly detection** modules. **Quality assurance** processes are embedded in the **life-cycle deployment framework**, ensuring consistency across stages from device modeling to operational monitoring. The framework’s reliance on **MDE principles** and **standardized extension points** further supports **interoperability** and **reproducibility**, though specific fidelity metrics (e.g., temporal resolution, spatial accuracy) are not explicitly quantified in the provided context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **heterogeneous, interoperable communication** across building systems, IoT devices, and cloud/edge platforms. **Communication protocols** are not explicitly named but must include **industry-standard options** (e.g., OPC UA for machine-to-machine, MQTT for lightweight IoT, or HTTP/REST for web services) to ensure **scalability and interoperability**. The **network topology** is inferred to be a **hybrid architecture**, combining **edge nodes** (for low-latency local processing) and **central cloud hubs** (for global analytics and storage). **Bandwidth requirements** are implied to be **high** to accommodate real-time data streams from dense sensor networks, with **latency constraints** prioritizing **sub-second response times** for critical control loops (e.g., safety systems). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are addressed through the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, emphasizing **fault tolerance** (e.g., failover protocols, data replication) and **accessibility** for diverse users. **Security protocols** include **authentication/authorization** (e.g., role-based access control for Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers) and **data encryption** (e.g., TLS for network traffic) to protect against unauthorized access or tampering. The system’s **modular architecture** with standardized extension points allows for **plug-and-play integration** of third-party network services (e.g., cybersecurity middleware)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **multi-tier hosting models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** options, tailored to the specific needs of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) domain. **Computational requirements** are dynamic, scaling from **lightweight edge processing** (for real-time local analytics) to **high-performance cloud clusters** (for large-scale simulations or historical data analysis). **Storage requirements** are optimized for **high-velocity data ingestion** (e.g., time-series databases for sensor logs) and **long-term archival** (e.g., BIM model versions, operational logs). **Scalability mechanisms** are enabled by the **modular architecture**, allowing **horizontal scaling** of components (e.g., adding more analytics services or data nodes) without full redeployment, as supported by **runtime configuration management**. **Deployment models** include **hybrid cloud-edge setups**, where **BIM-enriched models** are initially processed on-premise by **Building Control Engineers**, while **operational DTs** are deployed in cloud or edge environments for **Facility Managers** to interact with. **Availability and reliability assurance** are addressed through the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, ensuring **high uptime** (e.g., via redundant hosting zones) and **low-overhead maintainability** (e.g., automated updates, minimal downtime for patches). The **DT Framework** acts as a **central hub**, abstracting underlying infrastructure complexities to provide a unified interface for all stakeholders."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a non-functional requirement (NF_X), ensuring seamless integration with external systems via **standardized extension points** and **modular architecture**. Data exchange protocols are not explicitly specified, but the bidirectional synchronization between physical buildings and digital counterparts implies adherence to industry standards (e.g., BIM collaboration formats like IFC or COBie for static data, and OPC UA/MTConnect for real-time sensor/device data). Dependencies on external systems are managed through the **DT Framework’s central hub**, which acts as a mediator for enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS) or other Digital Twins. Integration patterns include **service-oriented architectures (SOA)** for analytics and **runtime-configurable plugins** for third-party tools, though exact protocols or APIs are not detailed in the provided documents."
]
